danger_signals.visual_form: Animated flame sprites and burning terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Active hazard requiring extinguishing; core threat mechanic

danger_signals.visual_form: Expanding fire graphics across level geometry

safety_signals.visual_form: Charred/darkened terrain where fires were suppressed
Likely visual indicators of water sources or refill stations

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fire-fighting equipment, hoses, water management visual assets

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visible flames, smoke, burning structures throughout level
Gradual reduction of flame sprites; transition to charred/dark areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level hazards diminish as player extinguishes fires

environmental_storytelling.example: Placement and intensity of flames communicates which areas are most dangerous or critical to address first
Burnt/charred environment shows fire's destructive impact and player progress in containment

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spreads/advances toward player and across combustible terrain
Retreats/diminishes when hosed; converted to safe (charred) terrain

notes: The Firemen uses a core mechanic of fire as the primary world-state communicator. The game lacks traditional faction conflicts or political elements—instead, the manifestation layer is dominated by fire/water dynamics and environmental emergency communication. Danger is signaled purely through flame presence and spread patterns. Safety is established through successful fire suppression and cleared zones. The game's visual language centers on emergency/crisis state rather than faction identity or political positioning.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor article on The Firemen)
